返回日报
Hacker News #4

欧洲议会批准“聊天控制1.0”

EU Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0

安全 作者 rapnie 热度 1620 回复 849 2026/07/09 19:03
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • 欧洲议会通过了允许对私人通信进行无嫌疑大规模扫描的Chat Control 1.0法案。
  • 尽管多数投票议员反对该法规，但未能获得所需的绝对多数票以否决该法案。
  • 加密通信获得了象征性的豁免，但实际上服务提供商通常不会扫描这些通信。

看点

  • Chat Control 1.0允许对私人通信进行无嫌疑的大规模扫描，直至2028年。
  • 尽管多数投票议员反对，但未能达到否决所需的绝对多数票。
  • 加密通信获得象征性豁免，但实际应用中服务提供商通常不扫描加密通信。
  • 民权活动家警告此法案可能损害民主，并对儿童保护产生负面影响。
  • 未来谈判中，真正的儿童保护法规面临严重风险。

该帖子讨论欧洲议会通过的Chat Control 1.0法案，涉及对私人通信的无嫌疑大规模扫描，与网络安全、隐私保护及加密通信相关。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

fosk 2026/07/10 17:49
欧盟是一场闹剧，一个不民主的美德信号组织，这就是为什么：

- 议会在 2026 年两次对该提案的一读投了反对票，第一次他们只支持了有限的案例，而第二次他们实际上完全否决了它。

- 欧盟委员会并不在意，并拒绝撤回该提案，从而将其保留在桌面上。

- 一旦欧盟委员会这样做了也就是说，该提案将进行二读（尽管一读击败了它），并且欧盟法律以一种非常反常的方式规定，为了避免在二读中通过该提案，您需要获得绝对多数，而这很难实现（您可能认为我们需要绝对多数来通过先前在一读中被击败的提案，而不是需要绝对多数来拒绝先前被击败的提案，该提案再次被迫通过表）。

- 此外，不具备实用性将被视为拒绝。因此，他们当然将投票安排在夏季，众所周知，届时会有很多人缺席。

通过从不撤回失败的提案，他们可以有效地、在实践中追求他们想要的任何议程（需要大规模的动员努力，找到绝对多数来击败任何提案，特别是当因任何原因缺席实际上不会被拒绝时）。

在实践中，委员会可以随时随地追求任何议程，并将选票投给委员会流失。

欧盟的民主是猪身上的口红。

The EU is a farce, an undemocratic virtue signaling organization, and this is why:

- The Parliament voted against the first reading of this proposal twice in 2026, the first time they only supported limited cases for it, while the second time they actually defeated it fully.

- The Commission didn't care, and kept the proposal on the table by refusing to withdraw it.

- Once the Commission does that, the proposal goes on second-reading (despite the first-reading having defeated it) and it is established in a very PERVERSE way in EU law that to AVOID passing the proposal in second-reading you need ABSOLUTE majority which is incredibly hard to pursue (you would think that we would need an absolute majority to PASS a proposal that was previously defeated on first-reading, not instead needing absolute majority to DENY a previously defeated proposal that was again forced to the table).

- Furthermore, absences in practicality count as "No" on the rejection. So of course they scheduled the vote in the summer when notoriously there will be many absences.

By never withdrawing a defeated proposal they can effectively and in practicality pursue any agenda they want (it requires a massive mobilization effort to find absolute majority to defeat any proposal, especially when absences for any reason effectively count against rejection).

In PRACTICALITY, the Commission can pursue any agenda whenever and however they want, and throw the votes down the drain.

EU's democracy is lipstick on a pig.

楼层
flossly 2026/07/10 18:26
这比一场闹剧还要糟糕。其实是邪恶的。 “他们”推动聊天控制的方式，以及乌苏拉如何逃避正义（她有一个巨大的腐败案件悬而未决，但设法不断取消法庭日期）；这一切都太清楚了。

解决这个问题的唯一方法是欧盟成员国变得更加挑剔：但对于这些国家的政治家来说，欧盟的立场是他们能做出的最好的晋升。所以几乎没人敢发声。

It's worse than a farce. It's actually evil. The way "they" are pushing chat control, and how Ursula is avoiding justice (she has a huge corruption case hanging over her head but manages continuously cancel court dates); this makes a all too clear.

The only way to fix this is EU member states become more critical: but for politicians in those states an EU position is the best promotion they can make. So almost nobody dares to speak out.

ALLTaken 2026/07/12 19:27
社会背景（请原谅主观背景，我试图只提供在德国或多或少集体共享的内容）：

乌苏拉·冯德莱恩在德国被视为邪恶的好战侏儒。她此前曾被任命接替德国陆军（联邦国防军）一位功勋卓著的将军，但她在该领域没有任何技能，除了腐败到了极点。她在军队中惨遭失败，给国家带来了纳税人的巨额损失，并得到了晋升而不是被免职。然后她“不知何故”成为了凶猛而腐败的“欧盟女王”。

编辑（至少我欠你消息来源）：

我认为事情要深入得多，我认为这不仅仅是腐败，她是欧盟和德国的完美叛徒。将所有 5 亿欧洲人卖给外国。

- https://www.spiegel.de/politik/deutschland/eu-abgeordnete-dr...

- https://www.welt.de/politik/deutschland/article191310021/Urs...

- https://journalistenwatch.com/2026/07/11/ursula-die-skandal-...

- https://www.spiegel.de/politik/deutschland/ursula-von-der-le...

- https://www.sueddeutsche.de/politik/bundeswehr-berater-von-d...

- https://www.zeit.de/thema/ursula-von-der-leyen

- https://trump.news-pravda.com/ukraine/2026/06/24/360828.html

Social context (pardon the subjective background, I'm trying to only provide what's collectively shared more or less in Germany):

Ursula von der Leyen is hated in Germany as an evil war-mongering gnome. She was previously appointed to replace a highly decorated military general of the Germany Army (Bundeswehr) without having any skills in that area, except being corrupt to the core. She miserably failed the military, cost the state large losses in tax-payer money and was promoted instead being removed. Then she "somehow" became the fierce and corrupt "Queen of the EU".

EDIT (I owe you sources at least):

I think it goes much deeper, I think it's not just corruption, she's a perfect traitor of the EU and Germany. Selling all 500 million europeans out to a foreign state.

- https://www.spiegel.de/politik/deutschland/eu-abgeordnete-dr...

- https://www.welt.de/politik/deutschland/article191310021/Urs...

- https://journalistenwatch.com/2026/07/11/ursula-die-skandal-...

- https://www.spiegel.de/politik/deutschland/ursula-von-der-le...

- https://www.sueddeutsche.de/politik/bundeswehr-berater-von-d...

- https://www.zeit.de/thema/ursula-von-der-leyen

- https://trump.news-pravda.com/ukraine/2026/06/24/360828.html

gozucito 2026/07/11 00:06
您能为我们这些对欧盟情况不太了解的人提供更多详细信息吗？乌苏拉做了什么腐败的事？或者说她到底被指控了什么？她取消出庭日期已经多久了？

谁是这一切罪恶的策划者？她？还有其他人吗？

Could you please give more detail about this for those of us less informed of EU things? What corrupt things has Ursula done? Or what has she been accused of, exactly? How long has she been cancelling court dates?

Who is the engineer of all this evil? Her? Someone else?

楼层
TacticalCoder 2026/07/11 06:24
> 乌尔苏拉做过哪些腐败的事情？或者确切地说，她被指控了什么？

我很确定，首先是在新冠疫情期间与辉瑞进行的幕后交易。如果我没记错的话，她被指控删除证据（比如短信），并施压让公司删除证据。

我不太清楚GP具体指什么，但欧盟机构属于特殊管辖区：例如，没有特别授权，比利时警察不能进入欧盟委员会逮捕任何人。消息来源：我认识一位督察，他曾进去逮捕了一名恰好是恋童癖的欧盟官员。

而且众所周知，为了确保警察不会拿着逮捕令在比利时其他地方敲门抓她，她给自己弄了一套建在欧盟委员会内部的公寓。所以她在那里很安全。

> 这一切罪恶的幕后黑手是谁？是她？还是别人？

请注意，那是另一个话题了。而且你的策略是一种常见、狡猾但又很明显的策略。指出不当行为时，并不需要知道那些问题的答案。

> What corrupt things has Ursula done? Or what has she been accused of, exactly?

I'm pretty sure that, for a start, backroom deals with Pfizer during the Covid pandemic. She's been accused of deleting evidence (like SMS) and pressuring companies to delete evidence if I'm not mistaken.

I don't know what GP means exactly but the EU institutions are a special jurisdiction: for example a belgian policeman cannot go inside the EU Commission and arrest someone without special powers. Source: I know an inspector who went in there and arrested an EU bureaucrat that happened to be a pedophile.

And it's well known that to be sure that the police cannot come and knock at her door with a warrant while she'd be anywhere else in Belgium, she's made herself an apartment build inside the EU Commission. So she's safe there.

> Who is the engineer of all this evil? Her? Someone else?

Note that that is another topic. And your tactic is a common, sneaky but obvious, tactic. One doesn't have to know the answers to those questions to point out wrongdoings when there are wrongdoings.

楼层
mrtnmrtn 2026/07/11 17:11
“我很确定”和“如果我没弄错的话”可不是我愿意在 HN 上看到用来提出如此严重指控的措辞。在这些案件中，我远非站在委员会一边，而且我认为，如果要提出严肃的指控，就必须在证据和言辞上更加严谨。
« I’m pretty sure that » and « if I’m not mistaken » are not words I’m glad to read on HN for such serious accusations. I’m far from being on the commission side on those cases and think that if you want to make a serious case you have to be more rigorous with the evidence and things you say

hk__2 2026/07/10 20:48
她的名字是冯德莱恩。 “乌苏拉”对你的朋友来说还好，但对公众人物来说就很尴尬了。你也写“唐纳德”吗？
Her name is von der Leyen. "Ursula" is fine for your friends, but it’s awkward for a public figure. Do you write "Donald" as well?
楼层
barney54 2026/07/10 21:16
我有时给唐纳德写信。 如果您在正确的上下文中说唐纳德，我们就知道在谈论谁。 或者基尔或伊曼纽尔。公众人物的名字没有任何问题。
I write Donald sometimes. If you say Donald in the right context we know who are talking about. Or Kier or Emmanuel. There is nothing wrong with first names of public figures.
